texto: IMPROVIDENCEThe other lives I might have ledAll now might as well beDead. Survived by no one.Barren, without issue of any sort:This withered bud, failedIn art and love. With no time leftTo change my course. But time enoughfor infinite remorse.
explicacao: Citação de John Tottenham sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “IMPROVIDENCEThe other lives I might have ledAll now might as well beDead. Survived by no one.Barr…” articula amor, improvidence, poetry em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e poetry.

frase_original: IMPROVIDENCEThe other lives I might have ledAll now might as well beDead. Survived by no one.Barren, without issue of any sort:This withered bud, failedIn art and love. With no time leftTo change my course. But time enoughfor infinite remorse.

texto: VIEW FROM A HILLI am not yet quite over it.I am lying down on top of it.Surveying behind me a wastelandOf dried-up promise.While the lights below twinkleWith dull mocking uncertainty.There isn't much left to look forward to,And the looking forward of the past has been belied.
explicacao: Citação de John Tottenham sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “VIEW FROM A HILLI am not yet quite over it.I am lying down on top of it.Surveying behind me a was…” articula sucesso, getting-over-it, looking-back em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como looking-back e moving-on.

frase_original: VIEW FROM A HILLI am not yet quite over it.I am lying down on top of it.Surveying behind me a wastelandOf dried-up promise.While the lights below twinkleWith dull mocking uncertainty.There isn't much left to look forward to,And the looking forward of the past has been belied.

texto: SONG OF DAWNI saw the sun rise by accident.It was a horrible sight.Annoyed by its splendor, I sought refugein a moist pillow, and lay there, alone,at the dawn of another day,that brought me closer to another death,pondering the vanity of my solitude,the vanity of procrastination,and the tiresome inevitability of waking upagain the same person.It might still be possible to change,but obstinately I remain the same,hoping that others might take solacein my consistency.But perhaps they take no solace in it,perhaps they too find it tedious.
explicacao: Citação de John Tottenham sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “SONG OF DAWNI saw the sun rise by accident.It was a horrible sight.Annoyed by its splendor, I sou…” articula reflexao, desperation, regret em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e mudanca.

frase_original: SONG OF DAWNI saw the sun rise by accident.It was a horrible sight.Annoyed by its splendor, I sought refugein a moist pillow, and lay there, alone,at the dawn of another day,that brought me closer to another death,pondering the vanity of my solitude,the vanity of procrastination,and the tiresome inevitability of waking upagain the same person.It might still be possible to change,but obstinately I remain the same,hoping that others might take solacein my consistency.But perhaps they take no solace in it,perhaps they too find it tedious.
